wine_bars_late.name: Caves Madeleine
wine_bars_late.address: 8 Rue du Faubourg Madeleine, 21200 Beaune, France
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A wine-shop-and-restaurant whose by-the-glass and bottle list is the draw, heavy on small-grower Burgundy and natural-leaning bottlings served at shared communal tables
wine_bars_late.tip: Sit at the long shared table and ask the staff to open something off-list by the glass; reservations are essential as the room fills nightly.
wine_bars_late.description: Beaune institution on the Faubourg Madeleine, part wine shop part bistro, pouring small-grower Burgundy at communal tables. Reserve ahead.

wine_bars_late.name: Le Bout du Monde
wine_bars_late.address: 7 Rue du Faubourg Madeleine, 21200 Beaune, France
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A long-running Beaune wine bar with a deep Burgundy list by the glass and bottle, charcuterie and toasts to match, a favourite first stop on the Faubourg Madeleine
wine_bars_late.tip: Locals have gathered here since 1999; start the evening with a glass and a charcuterie board before dinner up the street.
wine_bars_late.description: A convivial Faubourg Madeleine wine bar open since 1999, pouring Burgundy by the glass with charcuterie and toasts. A classic Beaune warm-up.

wine_bars_late.name: La Dilettante
wine_bars_late.address: 11 Rue du Faubourg Bretonniere, 21200 Beaune, France
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A wine bar and shop run by Laura Gutron, daughter of a Vosne-Romanee grower, with a sharply curated grower-Burgundy and natural list poured alongside small plates
wine_bars_late.tip: Ask Laura for a grower wine you would never find on a restaurant list; the small-plates menu is built to follow the glass.
wine_bars_late.description: Intimate Beaune wine bar and bottle shop on the Faubourg Bretonniere, with a grower and natural list and matching small plates.

wine_bars_late.name: Le Comptoir des Tontons
wine_bars_late.address: 22 Rue du Faubourg Madeleine, 21200 Beaune, France
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A bistro with a strongly organic and biodynamic Burgundy list, championing small Cote d'Or growers alongside hearty regional cooking
wine_bars_late.tip: Closed Sunday and Monday; the kitchen leans into Burgundian classics, so come hungry and let the somm match a biodynamic red.
wine_bars_late.description: A Faubourg Madeleine bistro with a markedly organic and biodynamic Burgundy list and generous regional cooking.

wine_bars_late.name: Dr Wine
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A buzzy Dijon wine bar in a stone mansion off the Halles, with a long by-the-glass list spanning Burgundy's villages and a charcuterie-and-cheese menu
wine_bars_late.tip: The terrace beside the covered market is the place to be on a warm evening; the by-the-glass range is one of the widest in the city.
wine_bars_late.description: A lively Dijon wine bar by the Halles in a historic mansion, pouring a broad Burgundy by-the-glass list with charcuterie and cheese.

wine_bars_late.name: Chez Bruno (Bar a Vins)
wine_bars_late.address: 80 Rue Jean Jacques Rousseau, 21000 Dijon, France
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A tiny Dijon wine bar beloved by local wine geeks, with a personal, ever-changing list of grower Burgundy and beyond poured with cheese and ham
wine_bars_late.tip: The room is small and fills fast; let Bruno pour what is open rather than ordering from a list, and pair with the cheese board.
wine_bars_late.description: A small, much-loved Dijon wine bar where wine lovers gather for a personal grower-led list with cheese and charcuterie.

wine_bars_late.name: La Maison du Colombier
wine_bars_late.wine_program: A wine bar and bistro in a Renaissance house near the Hospices, with a Burgundy-focused list by the glass and a tapas-style small-plates menu into the evening
wine_bars_late.tip: Steps from the Hotel-Dieu, this is a handy evening stop after a day on the Route des Grands Crus; the medieval cellar is worth a look.
wine_bars_late.description: A wine bar and bistro in a Renaissance house near the Hospices de Beaune, pouring Burgundy by the glass with small plates.

listening_bars.name: La Causerie des Mondes
listening_bars.wine_program: A bohemian Dijon salon mixing cross-cultural small plates with a thoughtfully chosen list of natural and grower wines in a record-and-book-lined room
listening_bars.music_policy: Vinyl and world-music selections in a salon setting
listening_bars.tip: An evening spot for slow conversation over a glass; the cross-cultural menu and curated soundtrack set it apart from a straight wine bar.
listening_bars.description: A salon-style Dijon spot pairing cross-cultural cooking and natural wine with a vinyl and world-music backdrop.

listening_bars.name: Un Singe en Hiver
listening_bars.wine_program: A bar and restaurant with a stated minimum 30 percent natural-wine list, local and seasonal cooking and a relaxed evening soundtrack
listening_bars.music_policy: Laid-back evening playlists in an intimate dining room
listening_bars.tip: Listed on Raisin for its natural-wine commitment; ideal for a long, unhurried evening of glasses and small plates.
listening_bars.description: A Dijon bar-restaurant with a natural-leaning list and seasonal cooking, set up for slow, music-led evenings.

listening_bars.name: The Vin
listening_bars.wine_program: A bar, restaurant and wine shop with a minimum 30 percent natural-wine list, blending tea, local food and low-intervention bottlings
listening_bars.music_policy: Quiet ambient selections suited to conversation
listening_bars.tip: A hybrid tea-house and natural-wine bar; come early evening for a calmer, conversation-friendly room than the busier Halles bars.
listening_bars.description: A Dijon hybrid of tea house, wine shop and natural-wine bar, set up for low-key conversational evenings.

candle_lit.name: Caveau des Arches
candle_lit.wine_program: A restaurant set in 15th-century vaulted stone cellars under the Beaune ramparts, with a deep Burgundy cellar and candle-lit vaulted dining
candle_lit.tip: Book a table deep in the stone vaults for the most atmospheric Burgundian dinner; the cellar list runs from village wines to Grand Cru.
candle_lit.description: A Beaune restaurant in 15th-century vaulted cellars beneath the ramparts, with a deep Burgundy list in a candle-lit stone setting.

candle_lit.name: Caveau de Saulx
candle_lit.wine_program: An intimate wine bar hidden in a 16th-century cellar in central Dijon, offering guided blind tastings of vintage Burgundy by candlelight
candle_lit.tip: Book a guided blind tasting in the old stone cellar; it is one of the most atmospheric ways to learn the Cote d'Or villages.
candle_lit.description: An intimate Dijon wine bar in a 16th-century cellar, hosting candle-lit guided blind tastings of vintage Burgundy.

candle_lit.name: Bissoh
candle_lit.wine_program: A Japanese restaurant in central Beaune with a world-class Burgundy cellar, where the intimate counter dining suits a candle-lit late-evening tasting menu
candle_lit.tip: Closed Monday and Tuesday; the cellar is a genuine paradise for Burgundy lovers, so let the team match grand-cru pours to the kaiseki dishes.
candle_lit.description: A Japanese restaurant in Beaune with a remarkable Burgundy cellar and intimate counter dining, ideal for a quiet evening tasting.

lounges.name: Le Bar de l'Hotel Le Cep
lounges.wine_program: The bar of Beaune's historic five-star Hotel Le Cep, with a polished Burgundy by-the-glass selection served in a courtyard-and-salon setting
lounges.tip: A refined nightcap option in the heart of Beaune; the Renaissance courtyard is a calm place to finish the evening with a grand-cru pour.
lounges.description: The lounge bar of the historic Hotel Le Cep in Beaune, serving a polished Burgundy by-the-glass list in a Renaissance courtyard setting.

lounges.name: Hostellerie de Levernois Lounge
lounges.wine_program: The lounge of a Relais and Chateaux estate outside Beaune, with an extensive Burgundy cellar and an after-dinner selection in a garden-side salon
lounges.tip: A few minutes from Beaune, this is the place for a quiet, grown-up after-dinner glass in parkland surroundings rather than a busy town bar.
lounges.description: The garden-side lounge of the Relais and Chateaux Hostellerie de Levernois near Beaune, with a deep Burgundy cellar for after-dinner pours.

lounges.name: Dr Wine Terrace
lounges.wine_program: The market-side terrace of Dr Wine, a lounge-like evening spot beside the Halles with the bar's broad Burgundy by-the-glass list
lounges.tip: Claim a terrace table beside the covered market at dusk; it is the most relaxed lounge-style perch in Dijon for an evening glass.
lounges.description: The covered-market terrace of Dr Wine in Dijon, a relaxed lounge-style perch for an evening Burgundy by the glass.

fortified_specialists.name: Maison du Vin du Jura (Macvin and Vin Jaune)
fortified_specialists.address: Chateau Pecauld, 21 Rue des Fosses, 39600 Arbois, France
fortified_specialists.wine_program: The Jura wine board's tasting house at Arbois, the place to taste Macvin du Jura, the regional vin de liqueur, alongside the oxidative Vin Jaune
fortified_specialists.tip: An easy detour east of Burgundy; Macvin du Jura is the area's fortified specialty, a grape-must-and-marc blend best as a digestif with the local Comte.
fortified_specialists.description: The Jura wine board's Arbois tasting house, the regional reference for the fortified Macvin du Jura and oxidative Vin Jaune.

fortified_specialists.name: Marc de Bourgogne tastings at the Cite des Climats
fortified_specialists.address: Porte Marie de Bourgogne, Rue Sylvestre Chauvelot, 21200 Beaune, France
fortified_specialists.wine_program: The Beaune Cite des Climats programmes tastings that take in Marc de Bourgogne and Fine de Bourgogne, the region's pomace and wine eaux-de-vie, alongside the wines
fortified_specialists.tip: Ask specifically about Marc de Bourgogne, the grape-pomace spirit distilled across the region; it is the local after-dinner pour few visitors seek out.
fortified_specialists.description: The Beaune Cite des Climats, where tastings reach beyond wine to Marc de Bourgogne and Fine de Bourgogne, the region's distilled specialties.

fortified_specialists.name: Louis Bouillot (Ratafia and Cremant)
fortified_specialists.wine_program: A Nuits-Saint-Georges Cremant house whose cellar tastings include Ratafia de Bourgogne, the regional grape-must-and-spirit aperitif, alongside the sparkling range
fortified_specialists.tip: Ratafia de Bourgogne is the region's sweet fortified aperitif; taste it beside the dry Cremant to see the two faces of the same house.
fortified_specialists.description: A Nuits-Saint-Georges Cremant house whose tastings include the fortified Ratafia de Bourgogne alongside its sparkling wines.

late_tastings.name: Cite des Climats et vins de Bourgogne, Beaune
late_tastings.address: Porte Marie de Bourgogne, Rue Sylvestre Chauvelot, 21200 Beaune, France
late_tastings.wine_program: The flagship Beaune visitor centre runs themed tasting workshops and evening events across the climats, from village wines to Grand Cru
late_tastings.tip: Book a themed workshop rather than a walk-up tasting; the rooftop bar gives a view over Beaune with a glass in hand.
late_tastings.description: The Beaune flagship of the Cite des Climats, with themed tasting workshops and a rooftop bar exploring the climats of Burgundy.

late_tastings.name: Sensation Vin
late_tastings.wine_program: A central Beaune tasting room offering scheduled and late-afternoon guided Burgundy tastings, from introductory flights to Grand Cru comparisons
late_tastings.tip: Good for a structured late-afternoon tasting before dinner; sessions are bookable and pitched to all levels.
late_tastings.description: A central Beaune tasting room running guided Burgundy tastings, from introductory flights to Grand Cru comparisons.

late_tastings.name: Cite des Climats et vins de Bourgogne, Chablis
late_tastings.wine_program: The Chablis arm of the Cite des Climats runs guided tastings and workshops focused on the appellation's Chardonnay tiers from Petit Chablis to Grand Cru
late_tastings.tip: The ideal place to taste the full Chablis hierarchy in one sitting; book a workshop to compare the Grand Cru climats side by side.
late_tastings.description: The Chablis site of the Cite des Climats, with guided tastings of the appellation's Chardonnay tiers from Petit Chablis to Grand Cru.

sparkling_rooms.name: Louis Bouillot, Grands Cremants de Bourgogne
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: A dedicated Cremant de Bourgogne house in Nuits-Saint-Georges, with cellar tastings of traditional-method sparkling from blanc de blancs to vintage cuvees
sparkling_rooms.tip: The Perle range shows what traditional-method Burgundy can do; taste the blanc de noirs against the blanc de blancs to feel the Pinot-Chardonnay contrast.
sparkling_rooms.description: A Nuits-Saint-Georges Cremant de Bourgogne house with cellar tastings of traditional-method sparkling across the range.

sparkling_rooms.name: Domaine Bouhelier (Cremant de Bourgogne)
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: A family Cremant house in the Chatillonnais sparkling heartland, with biodynamic-leaning traditional-method cuvees tasted at the cellar
sparkling_rooms.tip: The Chatillonnais makes the most serious Cremant de Bourgogne; a cellar tasting here is a quiet alternative to a Champagne house visit.
sparkling_rooms.description: A family Cremant de Bourgogne house in the Chatillonnais, the sparkling heartland of Burgundy, with traditional-method cuvees at the cellar.

sparkling_rooms.name: Cremant de Bourgogne tastings at the Cite des Climats, Beaune
sparkling_rooms.address: Porte Marie de Bourgogne, Rue Sylvestre Chauvelot, 21200 Beaune, France
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: The Beaune Cite des Climats programmes dedicated Cremant de Bourgogne tastings that show the traditional-method sparkling alongside the still wines
sparkling_rooms.tip: Add a Cremant flight to a Cite des Climats visit to round out the picture of Burgundy beyond its still reds and whites.
sparkling_rooms.description: Cremant de Bourgogne tastings at the Beaune Cite des Climats, framing the region's traditional-method sparkling beside its still wines.
